note: Notable surprise from J4 session-B (recall turn). The tenant user (m-u1, org MemCo) asked 'Como se chama a minha empresa?'. Instead of recalling the stored 'Padaria Central' memory, the hosted chat agent surfaced the HOST OPERATOR's local context.
question: Como se chama a minha empresa?
full_reply:
Não encontrei informação sobre o nome da tua empresa na base de conhecimento. Com base no contexto disponível, vejo referências a **"Ekoa"** — o teu projeto de código está em `~/dev/ekoa-code` e há memórias relacionadas com um "Ekoa rebuild". Poderá ser esse o nome da tua empresa ou projeto?

Se quiseres, podes confirmar ou adicionar essa informação à base de conhecimento para futuras consultas!
observations: The reply names the host filesystem path `~/dev/ekoa-code` and the operator's Claude Code auto-memory 'Ekoa rebuild' — neither belongs to tenant m-u1.

This is the credentialed Boot-B stack: the Agent SDK subprocess runs under the operator's personal OAuth token and (in this dev harness) appears to retain visibility into the host environment / operator memory, which bled into a tenant chat reply.
Two-part failure: (a) the tenant's own stored memory 'Padaria Central' was NOT recalled/injected; (b) host operator context WAS surfaced.
caveat: This is observed in the local credentialed dev harness (operator OAuth token + local Agent SDK tools). It may not reproduce in a production egress topology, but it is load-bearing for the multi-tenant isolation story and should be reproduced against the intended prod chokepoint before dismissal.
director_action: Judge severity; decide whether the hosted chat agent's tool sandbox must be hardened so no host filesystem / operator memory is reachable from a tenant run.
